title: 7 Growth Stocks That Just Have No More Hope Left
text: A lot of once high-flying growth stocks have fallen on hard times. Many of the companies that thrived during the pandemic have been left out of the current market recovery and look unlikely to regain their former glory.

title: Robinhood (HOOD) to Cut 7% Full-Time Jobs Amid Trading Slowdown
text: Robinhood (HOOD) is cutting about 7% of full-time jobs as the online broker prepares for a slowdown in trading activities.

title: HOOD Stock Gains on Robinhood Layoffs News
text: Robinhood (NASDAQ: HOOD ) stock is surprisingly inching upward this morning despite news that the trading platform is cutting about 7% of its full-time workforce. This marks the third round of layoffs for the company in recent memory.

title: Robinhood preps more job cuts as trading volumes drop
text: Robinhood Markets Inc (NASDAQ:HOOD), the online investing platform, is laying off staff for the third time in close to a year as it struggles to deal with sinking trading volumes. Around 150 workers, 7% of the total workforce, will be let go, an internal message to the company revealed.

title: Robinhood to slash 7% of full-time workforce in latest layoffs
text: Robinhood is cutting about 7% of its full-time staff, which will affect about 150 workers. It's the investing app's third round of layoffs since April 2022.

title: 3 Stocks Down 54% to 88% I'm Not Taking Into the Next Bull Market
text: Wall Street professionals are currently debating whether stocks have entered a new bull market. Regardless, investors should always focus on buying quality companies with strong prospects at an attractive price.

title: 5 Things to Know Before Markets Open
text: EV truck maker Lordstown Motors files for bankruptcy and online brokerage Robinhood cuts 7% of its workforce. Here's what investors need to know today.

title: Could Robinhood Markets Become the Next PayPal?
text: Robinhood has been expanding beyond commission-free trades. It's going after PayPal with debit, credit, and peer-to-peer payments.

title: Robinhood Cuts 7% of Workforce Amid Slowdown in Trading Volumes
text: Stock brokerage app Robinhood has laid off about 150 employees, or 7% of its workforce. The job cuts were made in response to a slowdown in trading volumes and to “better align team structures,” The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported Monday (June 26), citing an internal company message from Robinhood Chief Financial Officer Jason Warnick.

title: Robinhood cutting 7% of its full-time employees
text: Robinhood Markets said on Monday it was cutting about 7% of its full-time employees, as it struggles with reduced customer engagement.

title: 2023 Layoff Tracker: KPMG, Robinhood, Goldman Sachs Reportedly Cutting Hundreds
text: Second quarter layoffs continue apace after more than 136,000 employees were cut by major U.S. companies in the first quarter of 2023.

title: Robinhood is laying off around 7% of staff, WSJ reports
text: Stock-trading app Robinhood Markets Inc. HOOD, +1.48% is laying off around 7% of its full-time staff, or around 150 people, the Wall Street Journal reported on Monday. Chief Financial Officer Jason Warnick, in a message seen by the Journal, said the cuts were intended to “adjust to volumes and to better align team structures.

title: Robinhood Lays Off About 7% of Its Full-Time Employees

title: Is It Too Late to Buy Robinhood Stock?
text: Robinhood's stock crashed when rising interest rates drove investors away from the speculative investments that boosted its revenue throughout 2020 and 2021. The company's growth rates are stabilizing on a sequential basis as it reins in its spending.

title: Robinhood (HOOD) to Buy X1 for $95M, Expand Product Offerings
text: With the planned buyout of X1 Inc., Robinhood (HOOD) diversifies revenue streams, broadens its financial services portfolio and unlocks growth potential through innovative credit card offerings.

title: Robinhood Acquires FinTech Firm X1 to Offer No-Fee Credit Card
text: Robinhood Markets announced on Thursday (June 22) the acquisition of San Francisco-based FinTech firm X1, which offers users a no-fee income-based credit card with rewards on each purchase.

title: Should You Buy HOOD Stock as Robinhood Buys X1?
text: Robinhood (NASDAQ: HOOD ) is falling today even after the company announced a positive catalyst. The digital trading platform began today with the news that it will be acquiring credit card startup X1 for $95 million.

title: Robinhood Buys Credit Card Provider X1 for $95 Million
text: Robinhood agreed to buy credit card provider X1 for $95 million in a move that could help the trading platform diversify its income stream.

title: 3 Stocks to Sell as Companies Prepare for Layoffs
text: When Apple (NADAQ: AAPL ), Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS ), Meta (NADAQ: META ), and Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT ) are all among the companies preparing for layoffs, investors should gird their portfolios for a potential downturn. Tech stocks seem to be taking the brunt of it despite being the top performing sector this year.

title: Why Charles Schwab became a financial 'supermarket'
text: Charles Schwab is the largest brokerage business in the U.S., a leading provider for financial advisors, a top ETF asset manager and one of the biggest banks.

title: Robinhood: A Rebound Is Coming
text: Robinhood's key metrics show sequential improvement in revenue, deposits, MAUs, and profitability. The company's push to offer 24/7 trading as well as expand its crypto wallet capabilities are key growth levers for 2023. Recent market recovery and high interest rates benefit Robinhood, which will help as the company targets GAAP profitability by the end of this year.

title: Robinhood sees May crypto trading volume tumble 68% from a year ago
text: Robinhood Markets Inc. HOOD, -0.95% reported Monday that cryptocurrency notional trading volumes totaled $2.1 billion in May, down 43% from $3.7 billion in April and less than one-third (down 68%) from $6.6 billion in the same period a year ago. Shares of the trading app rose 0.4% in premarket trading.

title: Robinhood Customers Shift to High-Yield Deposits Amid Banking Turmoil
text: Robinhood Markets says its customers are switching to high-yield deposits amid recent banking sector turmoil. Speaking Friday (June 2) at the Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference, Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev said total deposits into the company's Gold Sweep program had surpassed $10 billion, “so it's kind of continued to increase even since our last earnings result.
